Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts To Allow Inspection Of All Elevators At Defts' Property
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF EDITH KLIMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS TO ALLOW INSPECTION OF ALL ELEVATORS AT DEFENDANTS' PROPERTY.

APPEARANCES: Ian Zimmerman, Esq. of Law Offices of Ian Zimmerman 292-1450 for pltf & moving party; Bret R. Landess, Esq. of Oium Reyen & Pryor 392-8300 for deft & opposing party Kone Inc.; Jeane Struck, Esq. of Severson & Werson 677-5508 for defts & opposing parties Bernheim and Coast Counties Property Management.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - GRANTED. THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT APPEARS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. WHETHER SUCH EVIDENCE IS ULTIMATELY ADMITTED IS A MATTER TO BE DETERMINED AT TRIAL. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Separate Statement
Off Calendar Aug-16-2010 Off Calendar

Ntn Of Mtn & Mtn To Compelling Testimony At Deposition; Pts And Authorities; Declaration; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Jul-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF EDITH KLIMAN's Motion to Compel Testimony at Deposition is DENIED on the grounds that the testimony sought from Mr. Briganti is not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence in this lawsuit and defendant KONE has previously stated in its response to Special Interrogatory No. 64 that it does not have a policy which requires the inspection of the elevator pit during routine service calls. Judge: MONICA A. WILEY, not reported.

After hearing, PLAINTIFF EDITH KLIMAN's Motion To Allow Inspection And Reproduction Of Photographs And/Or Videos In The Possession Of The San Francisco Medical Examiner's Office IS GRANTED. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
